content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from ICM and RND methods in Topic A) can be repurposed to dynamically prioritize which new experiences an active learning agent stores in its memory, pruning low-curiosity redundant data and retaining high-surprise unlabeled samples that most improve generalization. This creates a self-sustaining active learning memory system that grows only with high-value data, avoiding the catastrophic forgetting and storage bloat plaguing 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent architectures.
